title: BACKND Function product overview
summary: Introduces the separate BACKND Function product used to implement backend-executed functions, while noting that it depends on BACKND Base and links back to Base-side function invocation from the client.
capabilities: distinguish BACKND Function from BACKND Base client APIs
identify the need for a separate function auth key and toolchain
route questions between function authoring and Base-side function execution
surface environment assumptions such as Windows tooling and .NET requirements
prerequisites: BACKND Base is already in use
Function product activation and auth key issuance are complete
The development environment satisfies the current official toolchain requirements
common_pitfalls: Assuming BACKND Function can be used independently of BACKND Base
Treating client-side function invocation docs as the same thing as backend function authoring docs
Generating toolchain instructions without verifying current official installers and versions
verification_strategy: Use https://docs.backnd.com plus the source route before presenting current Function setup steps or tool downloads.
Treat this topic as routing metadata, not as a substitute for current Function product requirements.
public_notes: Agents should verify current installer links, IDE support, and runtime requirements from the official Function docs before presenting them as current.
This repository only stores routing and summary metadata, not the official tool downloads.
